candidate_check.description: OL 9 must be configured so that the root account is the only account having unrestricted access to the system.

check_content_excerpt:
Verify that OL 9 configures only the "root" account to have a UID "0" assignment with the following command:

$ awk -F: '$3 == 0 {print $1}' /etc/passwd
root

If any accounts other than "root" have a UID of "0", this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Change the UID of any account on the system, other than root, that has a UID of "0". 

If the account is associated with system commands or applications, the UID should be changed to one greater than "0" but less than "1000". Otherwise, assign a UID of greater than "1000" that has not already been assigned.
title: OL 9 must be configured so that the root account is the only account having unrestricted access to the system.
